exemplar.title:
To what extent did the 2008 USA housing crisis negatively impact the
agricultural sector of Spain?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent did the 2008 USA housing crisis negatively impact the
agricultural sector of Spain?

strengths: Title clearly communicates geographic scope and thematic focus
Research question is explicit and largely focused
Introduction consistently reiterates and maintains focus on agriculture
weaknesses: Occasional drift into unemployment and poverty beyond agricultural scope
Methodology lacks detailed justification of data treatment and evaluation criteria
Research question broadens later in the analysis, diluting precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a clear, appropriately communicated topic and a focused research question, placing it solidly in the 5â6 band. Minor dilution of focus (inclusion of poverty and unemployment) and surfaceâlevel methodology justification prevent the top mark, warranting a score of 5.

strengths: Use of economic terminology (AD/AS, cyclical unemployment, price elasticity) is mostly accurate
Bibliography includes a range of relevant international reports and scholarly articles
Integration of up-to-date secondary sources (e.g., BIS 2023) enhances contextual depth
weaknesses: Minor inaccuracies in terminology (e.g., âunitary demandâ) and conceptual slips
Incomplete or duplicated bibliographic entries and inconsistent citation formatting
Typographical errors in institutional names (e.g., âBanco de ExpaÃ±aâ)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source selection is mostly relevant and knowledge is applied with some effectiveness, but inconsistencies in citation, minor conceptual errors and uneven integration prevent performance in the top band. A mid-band score of 3 accurately reflects this level.

strengths: Majority of research is relevant and well chosen (primary statistics, peer-reviewed work)
Critical comparison of contemporaneous and retrospective Banco de EspaÃ±a reports
Integration of quantitative data with theoretical models to support conclusions
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally relies on assertion rather than deeper quantitative testing
Redundant descriptive passages reduce overall critical sharpness
Causal chains sometimes lack data references and clearer evaluation of methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student develops a coherent argument with largely supported conclusions andshows solid critical insight. Research items are well selected, and argument structure is consistent with mid-band descriptors. Lacking the incisive evaluation and methodological depth for the highest marks, a score of 5 is appropriate.

strengths: Clear academic structure with coherent headings and a contents table
Well-chosen and correctly labelled diagrams integrated into the text
Visual elements effectively illustrate key economic shifts
weaknesses: Referencing style is inconsistent (mixed parenthetical notes, raw URLs, duplicate entries)
Indentation and figure numbering inconsistencies can confuse readers
Long paragraphs lack sub-headings, and some figure captions omit axis labels
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation is organized and includes high-quality visuals, meeting the top band in structure and imagery. However, inconsistent formatting and referencing prevent full marks, justifying a score of 3 out of 4.

strengths: Topic choice reflects some personal interest
weaknesses: No RPPF reflections or evaluative commentary on planning and challenges
Minimal evidence of personal engagement beyond conventional analysis
Lacks demonstration of intellectual initiative or original research ideas
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With no reflective RPPF and only minimal inferred engagement, the work meets only the lowest non-zero descriptors in this criterion. A score of 1 accurately reflects the limited evidence of engagement.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure moves from macro context to sector-specific effects, and contrasting historiographical perspectives are reviewed, showing some critical insight.  Nevertheless, interdisciplinary evaluation (history + economics) is not deeply nuanced and transitions occasionally lapse.  Thus the work aligns with a solid but not exceptional argument, warranting 5.

Inâtext citations appear but formatting style is inconsistent (mixture of parenthetical notes and raw URLs, duplicate entries, lack of publisher details).  The bibliography is present yet uneven in style.  This fits âreferencing present but format varies,â giving 2.

The title clearly states both the geographic scope (Spain) and the thematic focus (impact of the 2008 U.S. housing crisis on agriculture).  Throughout the introduction the student consistently reiterates this focus, distinguishing it from broader recessionary effects.  The presence of an explicit global theme (âIB Environmental and/or Economic Sustainabilityâ) and a concise explanation of relevance show appropriate communication, although the essay occasionally drifts into employment and poverty beyond agriculture.  Overall clarity and suitability place the work solidly in the 5-6 band; a score of 5 reflects some minor dilution of focus.

Headings, sub-sections and a contents table give the essay a clear academic structure, and sections flow in a logical order.  Minor inconsistencies (duplicate conclusions, some long uninterrupted blocks of text) prevent full marks within the band; therefore 3 is suitable.

The essay demonstrates sound factual knowledge of the 2008 crisis, Spainâs macro indicators and elasticity concepts, and applies them to diagrams and data tables.  Integration is uneven: some claims about poverty and unemployment lack direct linkage to agriculture, and occasional conceptual slips (confusing price and income elasticity) appear.  Hence performance is âclearâ¦with some effectiveness,â meriting a mid-band score of 3.
No RPPF reflections were provided in the submission.  In the absence of evidence, only minimal descriptive engagement can be inferred, so the lowest non-zero band (1) is applied.

The student integrates data (GDP, unemployment, sales figures) with theoretical models to reach reasoned conclusions about shifts in demand and employment.  Analysis is generally coherent and supported, though causal chains occasionally rely on assertion rather than quantitative testing.  This matches âlargely supported conclusions,â so a mid-band 5 is defensible.

The research questionââTo what extent did the 2008 US housing crisis negatively impact the agricultural sector of Spain?ââis explicitly stated and largely focused.  However, later in the essay a second formulation adds unemployment and poverty, signalling a partial broadening that slightly compromises precision.  Because the question is clear yet not completely tight, the strand fits the 3-4 descriptor; the stronger end (4) is merited.

The majority of research items are relevant and reasonably well chosen, spanning primary statistics and peer-reviewed work.  There is evidence of comparing early and later Banco de EspaÃ±a reports and acknowledging bias.  While not comprehensive enough for the ârigorously selectedâ top band, it comfortably exceeds the minimal threshold, earning 5 within the 4-6 band.

Similarly, without the planning/decision reflections, intellectual initiative can only be judged as minimal.  The analysis itself is conventional and derivative of published sources, so a score of 1 in this strand is appropriate.

The student identifies primary macroeconomic statistics, centralâbank reports and academic articles and briefly justifies their use.  Methodological description, however, remains surface-level: there is no explicit explanation of data treatment, interdisciplinary framework or criteria for evaluating reliability beyond isolated source comments.  Selection appears generally appropriate but not âeffective and informedâ in the sense required for the top band, so 3 within the 3-4 range is appropriate.

The bibliography shows a mixture of international reports (IMF, BIS), Spanish statistics (INE, MAPA) and scholarly articles that are broadly relevant to housing, macroeconomics and agriculture.  A few citations are duplicated, hyperlink-heavy and lack full details, but most material is on-topic.  This aligns with âmostly relevant and appropriate,â warranting the upper end (4) of the 3-4 band.

Because the RPPF is missing, there is no direct demonstration of personal engagement.  The essay shows some interest through topic choice but cannot evidence more than minimal engagement, warranting a 1.

Three demand/supply or AD/AS diagrams are neatly drawn, correctly labelled and referenced in text, fulfilling the criterion for âwell-chosen, correctly labelled visuals.â  Hence a 3 within the top presentation band is appropriate.

Economic terms such as AD/AS, cyclical unemployment, sticky prices, price elasticity of demand are used largely correctly and consistently.  Minor inaccuracies (e.g., wording around âunitary demandâ) and occasional generic phrasing in historical discussion prevent a higher award, but overall terminology is âmostly accurate,â placing it at 3.

annotations.comment: The title concisely identifies both the geographic scope (Spain) and thematic focus (US housing crisis impact on agriculture), establishing a clear and appropriate topic communication.
The table of contents provides clear academic structure and navigation via headings; this organization enhances coherence.
Indentation levels in the contents table are inconsistent, which may confuse the hierarchy of sub-sections; standardize spacing to improve readability.
The research question is explicit and focused, yet the later inclusion of unemployment and poverty broadens its scope. Refine language to maintain precision.
Including the word count at 3998 demonstrates adherence to the IB limit and meets formatting requirements.
The paragraph repeating global vulnerabilities (p.3) is descriptive but does not introduce new analysis; consider trimming redundancy to maintain focus.
The opening paragraph outlines the crisis origin but could more explicitly link the U.S. event to specific agricultural outcomes in Spain; add a sentence clarifying this causal pathway.
The Carballo-Cruz quote effectively supports the view on external impact; this primary source choice strengthens the historiographical analysis.
The comparison between the 2008 and 2012 Banco de EspaÃ±a reports demonstrates critical source evaluation, illustrating how primary contemporaneous insights evolve with hindsight.
Highlighting institutional bias in the 2012 report offers solid critical insight into source reliability.
Sections on the BIS study are informative, but long paragraphs lack sub-headings; break text into thematic sub-sections for clarity.
The ADâAS diagram is well-labelled and directly referenced, effectively illustrating aggregate demand shifts in the Spanish economy.
Using specific unemployment percentages from the OECD strengthens factual grounding; continue integrating data points in subsequent sections.
The unemployment and CPI data table is effectively integrated, but the caption could specify years and indicators more explicitly.
The discussion of subprime mortgages (FCIC, 2011) lacks full bibliographic details; ensure complete inâtext citation matching the reference list format.
The initial methodology paragraph lists sources but lacks explanation of data treatment or evaluation criteria; expand justification of why each source is reliable and how data will be processed.
The research question in the analysis section broadens to include unemployment and poverty, diluting the original agricultural focus; align this restatement with the primary question.
The direct quotation from the Banco de EspaÃ±a report is insightful but lacks page reference parentheses; add âp. 38â to align with citation conventions.
The sentence concluding with âTheâ ends abruptly, suggesting missing content; ensure paragraphs are complete to maintain coherence.
While source bias is noted, quantitative methods used by Banco de EspaÃ±a are not evaluated; comment on statistical techniques or data limitations to deepen analysis.
Integrating the BIS 2023 findings showcases use of up-to-date secondary sources and enhances the contextual depth.
This discussion of domestic policy failures is thorough but insufficiently connected to agricultural impacts; explicitly link reforms to sectoral outcomes.
The text refers to âBanco de ExpaÃ±aâ with a typo; correct institutional names to maintain professionalism.
The high-value market diagram (Figure 3) lacks clear axis labels; add price and quantity axes titles for precision.
Repetition of âFigure 4â labels may confuse readers; ensure each figure has a unique number and caption.
The price elasticity section explains concepts well, but including the formula $$PED=\frac{\%\Delta Q_d}{\%\Delta P}$$ would deepen conceptual rigor.
The citation âAntennas and Vivasâ appears misspelled; verify author names to maintain academic accuracy.
The conclusion robustly synthesizes findings, but the phrase âburning government deficitsâ is unclear; rephrase for precision.
The standalone summary on p.19 reads as a conclusion fragment; integrate this statement into the main conclusion to avoid redundancy.
Text mentions a 4% drop in household income but this figure isnât in the table; include or reference the data source to support the claim.
The discussion of ILO vs INE sectoral unemployment illustrates critical comparison, highlighting methodological considerations.
